response.justification: Bogdan has nearly two decades of broad engineering experience and solid craftsmanship habits, but he is missing the core competencies this role is built around. The Principal Growth/Experimentation Systems position demands deep, demonstrable expertise in A/B testing platforms, telemetry infrastructure, experiment assignment and targeting, statistical/bias analysis, and cross-team systemic influence at enterprise scale — none of which appear meaningfully in his resume. His most relevant distributed-systems work (Dunnhumby, 2018–2020) is framed around latency and throughput, not measurement or experimentation. His seniority level reads as Senior/Staff IC rather than Principal IC6, with no evidence of leading multi-workstream initiatives or owning organization-wide technical strategy.
strengths: Nearly 20 years of polyglot experience spanning C, C++, Java, TypeScript, and more, covering most languages listed in the job requirements.
High-throughput distributed systems background at Dunnhumby — micro-services handling millions of HTTP requests per second with low-latency async I/O.
Strong testing culture and craftsmanship advocacy demonstrated through TDD, open-source testing tooling (Fluent-Asserts, Trial), and recurring community workshops.
concerns: Zero experimentation, A/B testing, feature flagging, or rollout-risk management experience anywhere in the resume — the central technical domain of this role.
No telemetry, metrics infrastructure, or data/statistical analysis work mentioned; the measurement and decision-making pillars of the role are entirely absent.

No demonstrated Principal-level scope: no evidence of leading multi-team initiatives, owning cross-product architecture, or delivering systemic platform capabilities used by multiple engineering teams.

key_factors.factor: No experimentation or growth systems experience
No telemetry, metrics, or statistical analysis background
Broad language coverage and high-throughput distributed systems exposure
